data.title: As an AI coding agent, I receive kudos for lint-clean Python commits, bug-referenced commits, and removing commented-out code
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement shell-based kudos triggers T6, T9, T12 — bug-ticket-referenced commits, lint-clean Python, and commented-out code removal — plus the end-to-end integration test verifying T9 through the full commit-validate.sh pipeline.
**Why**: These three triggers share no external dependencies beyond the stdlib/ruff tool already used in the project. T9 proves the entire end-to-end pipeline (ledger write, formula, ceiling, rank display) and serves as the walking skeleton trigger.
**Scope**:
- IN: T6, T9, T12 trigger implementations in commit-validate.sh; SC-E1-23 integration test
- OUT: sg-based triggers (Story E), AST-based triggers (Story F), review-workflow triggers (Story D)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T6 awards kudos when the commit message contains a bug ticket ID matching the project ticket format AND .claude/scripts/dso ticket show <id> confirms type=bug AND created_at predates git merge-base HEAD main; when the ticket cannot be resolved (not found, wrong type, or CLI error), T6 is skipped (fail-open: no award, no abort); the commit message regex pattern for ticket ID extraction is documented
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T6
- When this story is complete, T9 awards kudos when all staged Python files pass: git show ":$f" | ruff check --stdin-filename "$(pwd)/$f" -  (staged blob, absolute path, fixes ruff #17405 config-discovery); when ruff is not installed: T9 is skipped; when ruff fails with non-lint errors (config parse failure, internal error): T9 is skipped
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T9
- When this story is complete, T12 awards kudos when staged diff contains removed lines matching pattern ^\s*#\s*(def |class |import |from |if |for |while |return |raise |try:|except |with ) in removed diff hunks, with >= 2 consecutive matching lines required; single-line descriptive comments and docstrings are excluded
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-T12
- When this story is complete, an integration test creates an isolated git repo, stages a lint-clean Python file, invokes commit-validate.sh DIRECTLY (not via git commit), and asserts: T9 entry exists in ledger with points>0 AND stdout contains per-trigger breakdown; test uses test-batched.sh --runner=bash infrastructure
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-23
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
